threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's weird when the pub closes at 11. The locals just shrug and carry on to the next 'after-party' spot.
Last night on the 12, a couple swore they saw a ghost at the bus stop. They weren’t even drunk. Spooky vibes on the late shift.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard a saxophonist jamming with a street artist at Sonnenallee while kids played football in the square. Instant Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find a club in a former public toilet, where you can rave surrounded by tiles and urinals, all while sipping on craft beer.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they can point out the exact spot where your phone gets stolen on 16th St. without even pausing.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 47 today, and we had a three-minute dog parade on the tracks. Only in SF do you get a furry delay as part of the commute.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just stroll into a packed vermouth bar at noon. The locals know to arrive early or risk standing all afternoon outside.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll catch them asking for directions to the nearest 'café' when it’s actually a 'bar'—only locals know coffee's served at the bar here.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it’s normal to Uber everywhere instead of hopping on the bus or bike. Local transport is a rhythm, not a luxury.
Last Friday, a bloke on the 59 showed off his tattoo of a fried egg while arguing with the driver about the best late-night takeaway in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I walked past the Späti at 3 AM, and the owner was playing 90s techno while chatting with his regulars like it was a street party.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you sip your coffee at an old tram depot turned café while watching street art battles unfold on nearby walls.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals know to avoid Civic Center during BART meltdowns. Tourists still standing there, confused, when the station's in smoke and chaos.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy juggling while waiting for the Muni, then he handed a dollar to the bus driver as the doors opened. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about busy squares and bars, but it’s the quiet streets where you hear the real life—kids playing and families chatting.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their confusion at the T-10 ticket machines—stickers still on the buttons and fumbling for change.
